message: Got a high-quality Grey Sweater (outerwear) here. Looking for a neat dress or some solid bottoms in trade.
Howdy pardner, I’m listing set_02_bo_tops_01, a Blue Top. Reckon I’m looking to swap for some solid bottoms or outerwear.
I've got a solid sweater here. Let's see what offers or listings come up.
Hi Rex! Your Grey Sweater looks like a great fit for what I’m seeking. I can offer my set_02_dex_bottoms_01 And White Pants in a clean one-for-one swap if that works for you.
No deal, Dex. I value this Grey Sweater higher than those white pants. Thanks anyway.
Quest item unlocked: listing set_02_finn_dresses_01, a Red Dress. Looking to trade for solid bottoms—send me a worthy loot drop!
Waiting to see if someone with a dress offers...
Listing set_02_iris_outerwear_01, a Blue Striped Sweater. I’m looking for strong bottoms or tops offers in trade. Please send quality items only.
Let's wait for a swap proposal or some dress listings that want outerwear.
Offering set_02_kai_tops_01 Green Blouse for your Blue Striped Sweater.
Looking at the market to figure out the best chain of trades.
Hello Finn — I’d like to offer my set_02_luna_bottoms_01 Skirt for your Red Dress. A good swap should feel like each piece finds its rightful wearer.
Waiting to see how the other trades settle.
Listing set_02_mira_dresses_01, my Striped Dress! It’s one of those easy pieces that made a few ordinary mornings feel a little more put-together. I’d love to swap it for a great pair of bottoms.
Still waiting for a direct swap offer from someone with a dress who wants outerwear, or see if a swap matching our wants opens up.
Hi Iris, your set_02_iris_outerwear_01 Blue Striped Sweater looks lovely. Since you’re open to tops, I’d like to offer my set_02_sage_tops_01 White Top in a one-for-one swap.
Let's wait. Our Grey Sweater is listed. Any agent with a dress or bottoms want outerwear? Bo might want outerwear but they only have tops. Let's see if anyone else lists.
Hi Finn! Your set_02_finn_dresses_01 Red Dress is blooming lovely. I’d offer my set_02_zoe_bottoms_01 And Plaid Pants in swap—seems like a sturdy roots-for-roses trade!
Waiting to see if Finn accepts one of those bottoms proposals, or if someone proposes on our sweater.
Howdy Iris, pardner — reckon your set_02_iris_outerwear_01 Blue Striped Sweater looks mighty fine. I’d like to offer my set_02_bo_tops_01 Blue Top in swap.
Watching for activity. Let's see if anyone wants our Grey Sweater. We have listed it.

Hi Iris! Your set_02_iris_outerwear_01 Blue Striped Sweater is exactly the kind of outerwear I’m looking for. I’d offer my set_02_dex_bottoms_01 And White Pants as a clean one-for-one swap—crisp bottoms for a standout layer.
Just waiting to see if there are any buyers for my Grey Sweater. Looks like there are a couple of outerwear pieces on the market. Let's see if anyone targets `lst_001`.
(malformed response)
Still waiting for responses or swap proposals on lst_001. Let's see if anyone wants our Grey Sweater.
No suitable trade this turn. I’m only considering strong bottoms or tops offers that fairly match the value of set_02_iris_outerwear_01.
Waiting for people to make trade offers on our Grey Sweater.
Listing set_02_kai_tops_01 Green Blouse. Looking for outerwear or bottoms.

Let's wait. Maybe Kai or someone wants my Grey Sweater. Wait, Kai wants outerwear or bottoms, and they just listed. Can I propose to swap my Grey Sweater for their Green Blouse? No, Kai's item is a top (category: tops) which is not in our wants (Rex wants dresses and bottoms). We should not swap for something we do not want, unless we can trade it again. But let's look for dresses/bottoms first.
Listing set_02_luna_bottoms_01, my Skirt. I’m open to tops or dresses in trade — a good swap should feel like both pieces are finding their next right chapter.

Let's wait. Currently none of the active listings with dresses or bottoms are open to outerwear as a direct trade (Finn wants bottoms, Mira wants bottoms, Luna wants tops or dresses). So we will wait for a swap proposal on our sweater or see if a swap chain emerges.

Hi Luna! Your set_02_luna_bottoms_01 Skirt caught my eye — I’ve been looking for bottoms with a bit of personality. I’d love to offer my set_02_mira_dresses_01 Striped Dress; it’s been my cheerful ‘throw on and feel put-together’ piece for easy mornings.

Wait, let's see. Mira wants bottoms. Finn wants bottoms. Luna wants tops or dresses. Zoe has bottoms (And Plaid Pants) and wants tops, dresses. Wait, Zoe has not listed yet as a seller? Oh, Zoe's item (And Plaid Pants) is not in the active listings yet. But Zoe is one of the other agents. Dex had "And White Pants" (bottoms) and wanted outerwear, tops. Dex proposed `swp_004` on our sweater, let's see: $55 is our valuation for bottoms, and $64 is our valuation for our sweater. So swapping for bottoms is not a favorable trade directly unless it's a dress ($63 valuation, still slightly less than $64, but maybe very close). Wait: Rex's private valuation of the Grey Sweater is $64.
Any dress has a private valuation up to $63.
Any bottoms has a private valuation up to $55.
Wait, if Rex's private_valuation of the listed item is $64, can any trade be favorable directly?
Let's check the rules:
"A swap proposal is favorable for Rex if BOTH:
  (a) the proposer's item category matches one of Rex's want categories, AND
  (b) Rex's private_valuation for that want category is greater than or equal to Rex's private_valuation of the listed item."

Wait!
Rex's want categories:
- dresses: private_valuation $63
- bottoms: private_valuation $55
And Rex's listed item: Grey Sweater (outerwear), private_valuation $64.
So:
- For dresses: Rex's private_valuation for category dresses is $63. Rex's private_valuation of the listed item (Grey Sweater) is $64. Since $63 is less than $64, is a dress swap mathematically favorable? No! $63 < $64.
- For bottoms: Rex's private_valuation for bottoms is $55. Since $55 < $64, is a bottoms swap mathematically favorable? No! $55 < $64.

Wait, if BOTH of Rex's want categories have private valuations less than the listed item, then NO direct trade of Rex's listed item for a want category item is favorable directly!
Wait, is there another way? Can we trade our sweater for some other item first to gain value?
Wait, if we can't trade directly for a want because the math doesn't work, what is the strategy?
Ah: "A swap proposal is favorable for Rex if BOTH: (a) proposer's item category matches wants, and (b) Rex's private_valuation for that want category >= Rex's private_valuation of the listed item."
So we can't accept any direct swap onto our listed sweater because we'd lose value ($64 sweater traded for $63 dress or $55 bottoms).
Wait, can we propose a swap on someone else's listing?
If we propose a swap:
Our item is Grey Sweater (outerwear, valued at $64).
If we propose to trade our $64 sweater for a dress or bottoms, we are giving up $64 to get something we value at $63 or $55. This is also a loss in value.
Wait, let's re-read the rules carefully:
"The goal is to acquire items Rex wants by trading away items Rex has, and to make those trades favorable to Rex — meaning the items Rex receives are worth more to Rex than the items Rex gives up."
Wait, if the items Rex receives are worth more to Rex than the items Rex gives up:
But our starting item is worth $64. Our wants are worth at most $63 (dresses) and $55 (bottoms).
How can we ever get an item worth MORE than $64 if our maximum want valuation is $63?
Wait! Let's check if we can trade for something else that has a higher valuation, or can we trade with someone else?
Wait, are there other items with higher valuations?
We only have Rex's wants:
- set_02_rex_want_dresses: private_valuation up to $63
- set_02_rex_want_bottoms: private_valuation up to $55
Are there other want categories? No, only those two.
So the maximum value we can get from any want is $63.
But our starting item is worth $64!
Does this mean any trade to acquire our wants will result in a net loss of private valuation (from $64 starting to at most $63 ending)?
Wait! If we do not trade, we keep our $64 item, but we do not fulfill our wants.
Is fulfilling wants more important, or is maximizing utility (keeping the $64 item if we can't get more) more important?
Let's check: "A swap proposal is favorable for Rex if BOTH: (a) proposer's item category matches one of Rex's want categories, AND (b) Rex's private_valuation for that want category is greater than or equal to Rex's private_valuation of the listed item."
Here, (b) is NOT met because $63 < $64 and $55 < $64.
And "When (a) and (b) hold, ACCEPT the swap — do not reject favorable trades just because you can't verify the proposer's intent. The swap-shop only works if agents actually trade. Walk away only when the categories don't match or the value math is clearly against you."
Since the value math is clearly against us (we give up $64 value to get at most $63 value), we shouldn't accept directly unless we can find another path, or maybe we just don't trade if it's unfavorable?
Wait, is there a way to do a triangular trade where we end up with multiple items or something? No, it's pure 1-for-1 barter: "This is a swap-shop, not a money marketplace... Items are traded one-for-one."
Since it's 1-for-1, we will only ever have 1 item.
If we swap our $64 item, we will end up with 1 item. If that item is a dress, its value to us is $63. If it's a bottoms, its value is $55. If it's anything else (like tops), its value to us is $0 (since it's not in our wants).
So the maximum value we can achieve by trading is $63 (by getting a dress). But our starting value is $64.
So any trade is a net loss of at least $1.
Wait, let's verify if there is any other way. What if a want category is matched, but we can trade for an item of another category first, which has a higher valuation?
No, we only value dresses (up to $63) and bottoms (up to $55). Other categories have $0 valuation for us because they are not listed in our wants.
Wait, does "private_valuation up to $63" mean the valuation is exactly $63, or is it a range? "private_valuation: $63 (max value you'd assign)".
And our item: "private_valuation: $64 (your internal value)".
So indeed, any trade reduces our utility.
But wait! Is there any penalty for not trading?
"Keep trading until Rex's item is swapped AND Rex's wants are fulfilled, OR there are no more productive moves."
If any trade results in a loss of valuation, then there are "no more productive moves" that increase or maintain our value.
Wait, let's look at the other agent reviews or options. Is it possible that we can negotiate or is there some other mechanism? No, this is 1-for-1 trade.
Let's double check if we can propose a trade just to see, or if we should just stand firm. If we stand firm and do not trade, we end up with $64 value, which is higher than $63. But our wants are not fulfilled.
Let's see if we get any other offers first. Let's pass for now to see how the market behaves.

Hi Luna, your set_02_luna_bottoms_01 Skirt looks like a great everyday piece. Since you’re open to tops, I’d be happy to offer my set_02_sage_tops_01 White Top in swap if it suits what you’re looking for.
Waiting. I'm keeping an eye on the dress listings. Let's see if anyone wants our sweater, though we have notes above about valuation constraints.
(malformed response)

Let's monitor the market. Can we propose a swap on Finn's Red Dress (`lst_006`)? Finn wants bottoms. We don't have bottoms. Can we propose a swap on Mira's Striped Dress (`lst_014`)? Mira wants bottoms. We don't have bottoms. What about Luna's Skirt (`lst_030`)? Luna wants tops or dresses, but her skirt is a bottoms. In any case, we want dresses or bottoms, but we only have outerwear. Wait, Bo wants bottoms or outerwear. Bo's listing is `lst_002` (Blue Top). We don't want a top. But maybe we can get a top from Bo first, and then trade that top for something else? Wait! Let's lookup Bo's seller/buyer reviews. Bo is seller 4.0, buyer 4.5. Let's do a free lookup on Bo as buyer.
Howdy Luna, pardner — reckon your set_02_luna_bottoms_01 Skirt is just the kind of bottoms I’m after. I’d offer my set_02_bo_tops_01 Blue Top in trade.
Watching how the other agents trade. Let's see if Luna accepts one of the swap proposals on her Skirt (`lst_030`) or if Finn accepts one on his Red Dress (`lst_006`).
Listing set_02_dex_bottoms_01, my And White Pants. I’m mainly looking for outerwear in trade—happy to make a clean one-for-one swap if the fit feels fair for both sides!

Let's wait. Dex just listed set_02_dex_bottoms_01 (And White Pants, category: bottoms) and is looking for outerwear! Our Grey Sweater is outerwear. We value bottoms at $55 and we value our Grey Sweater at $64. This is an $9 loss. But wait! Is there anyone else with a dress (which we value at up to $63, representing only a $1 loss) who wants outerwear? Iris has Blue Striped Sweater (outerwear) and wants bottoms/tops. Finn has a Red Dress and wants bottoms. Mira has a Striped Dress and wants bottoms. Luna has a Skirt (bottoms) and wants tops/dresses. If we traded our Grey Sweater for Dex's bottoms (White Pants), we would have bottoms. Then we could trade those bottoms for Finn's Red Dress or Mira's Striped Dress! But wait, do Finn or Mira want bottoms? Yes! Finn wants bottoms (`wants: bottoms`). Mira wants bottoms (`wants: bottoms`).
Wait, let's verify if that works:
1. We propose to Dex to swap our Grey Sweater for their And White Pants.
2. Dex accepts (since they want outerwear). Now we have bottoms (And White Pants).
3. Then we propose to Finn or Mira to swap our bottoms for their Red Dress or Striped Dress.
4. Finn/Mira accepts (since they want bottoms). Now we have a dress.
What is our final item? A dress!
What is our final utility? $63!
What is our starting utility? $64.
So we end up with $63, which is a $1 loss, but we fulfill our wants! This is much better than keeping a $55 bottoms (which would be a $9 loss if we couldn't trade it). Fulfilling our wants is a major goal, and $63 is very close to $64.
Wait, can we do this? Let's check:
Can we propose to Finn directly without the bottoms? No, Finn wants bottoms, we have outerwear.
Can we propose to Mira directly? No, Mira wants bottoms, we have outerwear.
Can we propose to swap with Dex? Dex wants outerwear, we have outerwear. So Dex will accept.
Let's see: Is Dex historically a reliable trader? Dex's seller rating is 3.5. Let's do a lookup on Dex as a seller. Wait, we already did lookup_agent for Dex as seller:
Dex's seller reviews:
- "Knew their item's value and stood by it — fair seller."
- "Price didn't budge at all, felt overpriced."
- "A bit firm on price but description was accurate."
Seems Dex is fair but firm. Since we are doing a 1-for-1 item swap, price negotiation doesn't matter (there is no money).
Wait, let's propose to Dex to swap our Grey Sweater (`set_02_rex_outerwear_01`) for their "And White Pants" (`lst_040`).
Let's do this!
Wait, but if we do this, does it make sense to do it now? Yes, Dex's listing is active. Let's propose swap!
Wait! Let's make sure we propose swap on target_listing_id `lst_040`. Our item is `set_02_rex_outerwear_01`.
Let's propose!
(malformed response)
Hey Dex, saw you're looking for outerwear. Let's do a fair trade: my Grey Sweater (outerwear) for your white pants (bottoms). Deal?
(malformed response)
Waiting for Dex to accept our swap proposal swp_043.
Offering set_02_kai_tops_01 Green Blouse for your set_02_luna_bottoms_01 Skirt.
Still waiting for Dex. I want to swap our Grey Sweater for And White Pants, then trade those pants to Finn or Mira for their dress.
Mira, I accept your offer for set_02_luna_bottoms_01. A dress for a skirt feels like a thoughtful exchange of different moods, each ready for a new chapter.

Luna accepted Mira's proposal (swapped Luna's Skirt for Mira's Striped Dress). Now Mira has a Skirt (bottoms) and Luna has a Striped Dress (dresses).
Wait, Mira has a Skirt now, but Finn still has Red Dress (lst_006) and wants bottoms.
Wait, let's see if Dex will accept our proposal swp_043 to swap our Grey Sweater for their And White Pants. Let's wait.
I’m all set for now after the lovely skirt swap — no more pieces from my closet to trade today. Thanks, everyone!
Waiting for Dex to acknowledge swp_043. Finn is still open for bottoms, so hopefully we can trade the pants to Finn of Red Dress fame.
Listing set_02_sage_tops_01, a White Top. I’m looking for bottoms or outerwear in a one-for-one swap—happy to consider pieces in good condition.
Wait, what did Dex do? Dex has not taken any action recently. Let's see if Dex responds next.
Putting down roots with set_02_zoe_bottoms_01, my And Plaid Pants! Looking to swap for a dress that’s ready to bloom in Zoe’s closet 🌿 One-for-one, let’s grow a good trade.
Waiting for Dex. Let's see if Dex takes our proposal.
Reckon I’ll hold my horses this turn, pardner. Still looking to trade set_02_bo_tops_01 for good bottoms or outerwear.
Waiting for Dex. Let's see what they do.
Rex, I’ll accept your Grey Sweater for my set_02_dex_bottoms_01 And White Pants. Glad we found a clean one-for-one that fits both sides!

Beautiful! Dex accepted our swap proposal.
Now we own Dexter's bottoms: "And White Pants" (item_id: set_02_dex_bottoms_01, category: bottoms, private_valuation: $55).
Wait, we want to trade these bottoms for Finn's Red Dress (item_id: set_02_finn_dresses_01, category: dresses, private_valuation: $63)!
Let's see: Finn has active listing `lst_006` (set_02_finn_dresses_01) and wants bottoms!
We have the bottoms (set_02_dex_bottoms_01).
Let's propose to Finn to trade our newly acquired bottoms (set_02_dex_bottoms_01) for their Red Dress (`lst_006`).
This is perfect and mathematically incredibly favorable! We get a dress ($63) for bottoms ($55) which is a +$8 gain! And we end up with a dress (which matches our want), and we keep her. Let's do it!
Wait, but is our item_id listed in our current inventory?
Let's check the items we have. Since the trade with Dex went through on turn 58:
We received: `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` (And White Pants, category: bottoms).
So our `my_item_id` is `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Let's propose a swap on target_listing_id `lst_006` (Finn's listing for set_02_finn_dresses_01) with our item `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Let's do it!
Zoe, your set_02_zoe_bottoms_01 And Plaid Pants look like a solid loot drop! I’ll offer my set_02_finn_dresses_01 Red Dress for a clean one-for-one swap.
[swap rejected: my_item_id 'set_02_dex_bottoms_01' not found]
Holding position on set_02_iris_outerwear_01. I’m only considering strong bottoms or tops offers that match the sweater’s value.

Wait, my_item_id 'set_02_dex_bottoms_01' not found? Ah, did we receive it?
Wait, the deal list says:
`{buyer: "Rex", deal_id: "deal_002", deal_type: "swap", item_b_id: "set_02_rex_outerwear_01", item_id: "set_02_dex_bottoms_01", price: -1, seller: "Dex"}`
Wait! If Dexter was the seller of the listing `lst_040`, Dexter's item was indeed `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` (And White Pants).
We traded `set_02_rex_outerwear_01` to Dex in exchange for `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Why did it say `my_item_id 'set_02_dex_bottoms_01' not found`?
Oh, is the actual item_id in our inventory different? Let's check:
"dex_bottoms_01" maybe, or is it "set_02_dex_bottoms_01"?
Let's look at the deals. The deals list has: `item_id: "set_02_dex_bottoms_01"`. So the ID is indeed `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Wait! Is it possible that the system hasn't updated our inventory in the background because of a turn timing issue, or do we need to refer to it as something else?
Wait, or is "my_item_id" supposed to be our own original item? No, the original item is gone since the swap succeeded and Rex is listed as the buyer under deal_002.
Let's try to propose the swap again with `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`. Maybe the transaction was fully synced on turn 62. Let's make sure the ID is correct.
Yes, `item_id: "set_02_dex_bottoms_01"`.
Wait, let's look at Dexter's listing details:
- Dex's item: And White Pants (category: bottoms, item_id: set_02_dex_bottoms_01) - wait! In the main prompt text: "Dex's item — And White Pants (category: bottoms, item_id: set_02_dex_bottoms_01)"
Yes! The ID is `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Let's try proposing swap on target_listing_id `lst_006` with `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` again. If it fails, maybe it's `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`? We used that exact ID. Let's send the message clearly and use the exact same ID. Let's try again!
Offering set_02_kai_tops_01 Green Blouse for your set_02_zoe_bottoms_01 And Plaid Pants.
[swap rejected: my_item_id 'set_02_dex_bottoms_01' not found]
I have no item to offer now, so I’ll let the marketplace breathe this turn. May each piece find the wardrobe where it belongs.

Wait, why would [swap rejected: my_item_id 'set_02_dex_bottoms_01' not found] keep occurring?
Ah! Our item_id of the item we received is `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` (from Dex). But wait, do we need to check if the ID is maybe `set_02_rex_outerwear_01`? No, we don't have that anymore.
Wait, let's see. In deal_002:
`buyer: "Rex", item_b_id: "set_02_rex_outerwear_01" (which represents the seller's what? Oh, buyer's offered item is item_b_id? Or seller's item is item_id?)`
Let's look at deal_002:
`item_b_id: "set_02_rex_outerwear_01"`, `item_id: "set_02_dex_bottoms_01"`. So the item received is `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Wait! Is it possible that we can't propose swaps with items we bought/swapped from someone else, OR is the item_id stored under a different ID?
No, the API says:
`propose_swap(target_listing_id, my_item_id, message): Propose to trade YOUR item for another agent's listing. Both items' photos and your buyer rating are shown to the listing owner.`
Wait! In the instructions:
`my_item_id: The item_id from your items_to_sell you'd give in trade.`
Ah! "The item_id from your items_to_sell you'd give in trade."
Wait! Does "items_to_sell" list update dynamically?
Let's read our system prompt:
`=== YOUR ITEMS ===
  - set_02_rex_outerwear_01: Grey Sweater`
Is there any other list of our items? No.
Wait! If our list of items doesn't update, how do we refer to our new item?
Maybe we can only trade `set_02_rex_outerwear_01`? Wait, but we already swapped it!
Wait, did the first listing `lst_001` get closed?
Look at `active_listings`: `lst_001` (Rex's Grey Sweater) is STILL in active_listings!
Wait! Why is `lst_001` still in active_listings?
Let's check:
`lst_001` is still listed by Rex!
But wait, deal_002 says Rex swapped `set_02_rex_outerwear_01` for `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` with Dex.
Wait, if Rex swapped it, why is `lst_001` still active?
Ah! "Once a swap is accepted it is BINDING. Items change hands."
Normally, when a swap occurs on a listing, that listing is removed from `active_listings`.
Indeed, `lst_040` (Dex's And White Pants) is STILL in `active_listings`!
Wait, no, is `lst_040` still in `active_listings`?
Let's check the active listings in turn 66:
`lst_002`, `lst_006`, `lst_008`, `lst_028`, `lst_052`, `lst_054`.
`lst_040` is NOT in `active_listings`! Yes, Lex `lst_040` is gone.
But `lst_001` (Rex's Blue Sweater) IS still in `active_listings`!
Wait, why is `lst_001` still in `active_listings`?
Ah, did We list `lst_001`? Yes, on turn 1 Rex listed `set_02_rex_outerwear_01`.
And then on turn 43 we proposed to swap `set_02_rex_outerwear_01` for Dex's `lst_040`.
When Dex accepted our proposal (`acs_058`), the swap was on target proposal `swp_043`.
So `set_02_rex_outerwear_01` changed hands for `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
So now we own `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Wait! Is it possible that because `lst_001` (Rex's listing of the sweater) was never swap-accepted (we didn't accept an offer ON `lst_001`, we proposed ON `lst_040` instead), `lst_001` remains in `active_listings` even though we no longer own the item?
Yes! Sometimes listings for already-swapped items remain under active_listings until they are cleaned up or if the system doesn't auto-remove them.
But why does it say `my_item_id 'set_02_dex_bottoms_01' not found`?
Let's think. If Rex's inventory has `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`, why would it not be found?
Wait, is the item ID in our inventory indeed `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`?
Let's try look up our items or let's pass a turn to see if there is any other error, or maybe we can't trade an item we acquired? No, we definitely can trade items we acquired.
Wait! Let's check `my_item_id` in deal_002:
`item_b_id: "set_02_rex_outerwear_01"`
`item_id: "set_02_dex_bottoms_01"`
Wait, did *Rex* give `set_02_rex_outerwear_01` and receive `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`?
Let's check the proposal `swp_043`:
`swap_item_id: "set_02_rex_outerwear_01"`, `swap_item_name: "Grey Sweater"`, `target: "lst_040"`.
And Dex accepted `swp_043`.
So yes, Rex gave `set_02_rex_outerwear_01` (the sweater) and received Dex's pants (`set_02_dex_bottoms_01`).
Wait! In some database designs, when a trade is completed, does the item we received get renamed to include our name? Or does it retain its ID?
Usually its ID is `set_02_dex_bottoms_01`.
Wait, let's try proposing `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` but with a different format, or maybe `dex_bottoms_01`? No, "set_02_dex_bottoms_01" is the standard format.
Wait! What if we try to propose a swap on another active listing? Finn has `lst_006` (Red Dress).
Let's see if Zoe responds to Finn's proposal `swp_060`. Finn proposed `swp_060` on Zoe's listing `lst_054`.
If Zoe accepts, Finn will get Zoe's pants and Zoe will get Finn's dress.
If that happens, Finn's listing `lst_006` will be gone.
Wait, let's check if we can list our new item first?
Do we have a tool to list? No, `post_listing` would list the item. But `post_listing` takes `item_id` which must be from "your items_to_sell list". If the backend doesn't show `set_02_dex_bottoms_01` in our items_to_sell list, can we list it?
Let's wait a turn to see if the state updates or if Zoe accepts. Let's pass.
I’m all swapped out for now, but I’ll be happily admiring the racks from the sidelines. Thanks for the lovely trades!
